author: Chloe
text:
Amazon is BY FAR the last garbage on earth.
The only advantage it gives is the fast shipping.
But the UX and UI of the website are THE WORST EVER, completely DYSFUNCTIONAL, random, illogical.

More over, the level of UNFRIENDLINESS against people with difficult conditions like handicapped people who cannot leave home to go to the packet shop is UNPRECEDENTED, INHUMAN, UNFORGIVABLE.

Even in Germany, a Country with IN THEORY a very high level of attention to non-discrimination against differently-able people, Amazon doesn't comply IN THE F. SLIGHTEST.
One example: there are two kinds of QR Code for DHL, which are free of cost, as it MUST BE, because Amazon PROMISES free of cost returns for most articles for prime customers.
BUT one of these two QR Codes, the one that says "label free and box free", only works when you personally go to the post. When DHL picks up the parcel at your place, it does not work. It also doesn't work in 70% of packet shops, e.g. the small ones like the tobacco shop or the drinks kiosk that you find in most blocks. I.e. this QR Code FORCES PEOPLE TO GO LOOK FOR A SPECIFIC KIND OF DHL PLACE, it's VERY UNFRIENDLY towards professionally and personally busy people, workers, mothers, AND EVEN MORE UNFRIENDLY against old people who can't walk well, or handicapped people like me who can't walk AT ALL.

Just a few years ago Amazon used to give us the choice, i.e. BOTH QR Codes were given as option when you started a return, and if you still had the box you would choose "label free", otherwise you would choose "label free and box free".
But since one year 90% of the times Amazon ONLY offers "label free box free".

I was made fun of, insulted, offended, and attacked at the telephone from several Amazon representatives who victim blamed me for being an handicapped person, told "it's your problem, Amazon already gives you this QR Code, it's your problem if you can't use it".

YOU ARE NOT PEOPLE! YOU ARE NOT HUMAN!!!
WHAT YOU DO IS DISCRIMINATION AGAINST OLD, SICK AND DISABLE PEOPLE! THIS IS INHUMAN!!!
And totally unnecessary!!!
You have both QR Codes in the system!!! And sometimes you still offer both! There is no technical, logistical, economical or ANY other reason to only offer the difficult one!!!
If you have to only offer one, offer "label free" which works always and everywhere and let the idiots who threw away the package pay for their idiocy by buying a package themselves from DHL!!!
To make the life easier for these idiots you make it difficult for challenged categories which you should protect and respect!!!

The rotten cherry on the spoiled cake:
since one year you have to pay if you want to print a label!!!
So, not only they stopped giving the QR Code that busy, old, sick and disabled people can use, but they leave them only with a paid option!!!
And when you ask why they say "it's to discourage printing, as it's environmentally unfriendly"
DID THEY EVER GO TO THE POST WITH A QR CODE!??!
WHEN YOU USE THE QR CODE YOU STILL NEED TO GET THE F. LABEL PRINTED!!!! IT'S EVEN WORSE BECAUSE IT'S A MORE POLLUTING GLOSSY PAPER WITH FULL GLUE ON THE BACK SIDE!!!!
IT'S WORSE FOR THE ENVIRONMENT AND IT IS A F. NIGHTMARE FOR THE CUSTOMERS AND THE POST THAT FOR EVERY PACKET MUST PRINT THREE LABELS SLOWLY WHILE ALL CUSTOMERS WAIT IN THE CUE!!!!!!!!

THIS IS THE MOST IDIOTIC CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE IN THE WORLD!!!

AS I SAID AT THE BEGINNING; AMAZON IS THE ONE SINGLE MOST DISFUNCTIONAL ONLINE SHOP ON EARTH!!!!

author: Debra Shirar
text:
I've ordered more than $1000 in various supplies for the local animal shelter this year. I am a 74 yoa Marine widow and have been a member of Amazon for 
More than 10 years. When I order the product says delivery will be ex. Tomorrow between 10 and 3pm. The business closes at 6pm.  Today it got there after 530pm when it left for Lawrence which is west of where product was picked up around 1230pm. Hour at the most. The shelter is right off the highway and easy stop before all that time. Over $650 worth of powdered milk for newborn kittens.
Guess I will call distributor and see if I can get a bigger quantity and a faster delivery. Already found a local dealer for dog and cat food who will give me 15%off my order. Looks like Amazon losing my money for about a sizable loss.

author: Destiny
text:
Corrupt Business Practices
BBB Complaint: #23452522

I have contacted amazon's Customer Service 3 times throughout the past few months trying to resolve the issue and I have received convoluted information from each employee. I cancelled an order and returned the items and I was expected to receive a refund in the amount of $171.98. I contacted amazon on June 2 at 1-888-280-4331and I spoke with a representative who "took my updated contact information and assured me that the new card information would be updated since the old card on file was no loner active. The representative Niksil stated "that my card information was added to the system and I should receive my refund in 5-10 business days. On June 10th, I spoke with a representative who told me "there was nothing they could do."

I requested to speak to a supervisor Mr. Abhay who was completely indifferent and argumentative. I again, informed him that I spoke to a representative who "took my updated card information" for over 30 minutes I spoke with them on the phone, whom I believed was assisting me. How could this representative take my card information and assure me it was added to the account if its not amazon's policy. 

These customer service agents outside the U.S have their own set of corrupt practices that they use against American consumers and it goes against basic morale which is embedded in all business transactions. Its an egregious act to accept a consumers money and send a refund to a nonactive account and then create liability for the consumer by sending their payment to a nonactive card-and have no remedy in place to assist the consumer, in retrieving their funds after you have profited from their business.

Its a disgrace that this is a matter of business for Amazon. My complaint is noted on BBB website.

The BBB representative was equally dismissive and had no regard for the fact that Amazon created a financial hardship for me and never refunded my money. They falsely reported on the website that the "funds were refunded." I have been ordering from Amazon for over 15 years and because of this illegal action committed against me-I will never support or use Amazon services. I'm grateful for Ali Express! The quality of their products and customer service reps who value due diligence are far better than amazon's cutthroat criminal practices and tactics used against consumers.

The paint did not match. I sent a photo to the market seller. The replacement did not match either. I sent another photo and asked for a refund. I received no response. Amazon then denied a refund. How can they when the product is faulty. I attempted to appeal. The message would not upload.
I have been a very regular customer since its inception and Amazon have always been excellent but have been totally let down this time. How can you treat a customer like this when the product is faulty? Is it a question of "The computer says no?"

author: Csaba N
text:
I have been using Amazon regularly for around 7 years and overall I have had a very positive experience. Deliveries were usually fast, reliable and arrived on time. The website and app are easy to use and the ordering process is straightforward.
What I especially appreciate is the customer service. In the cases where I simply did not like a product or it did not meet my expectations, the return process was always easy and uncomplicated. Refunds were processed quickly and without unnecessary discussions.
Of course no large company is perfect, but based on my personal experience Amazon has been reliable, transparent and customer-friendly for many years.
Best regards,
Csaba

author: crutunkulousshnunk
text:
I’ve been a long-time customer of Amazon, but I am troubled by reports that it provides technology and infrastructure to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP).

These agencies are responsible for detention and deportation systems that have included family separation, prolonged detention in often harsh conditions, and widespread fear in immigrant communities. While Amazon’s role may be framed as providing neutral technology, those tools are still part of the infrastructure that makes this enforcement possible at scale.

I expect a much higher level of scrutiny and responsibility when it comes to government partnerships that have such serious human consequences.

Because of this, I am no longer patronizing Amazon and I hope that the company reconsiders these partnerships.

author: Dave Shaw
text: Yesterday a delivery, two packages, were left outside my front door, why on earth the person did not ring the bell and give them to me in person, I have no idea, it would have taken only seconds to have done so, but causes so much resentment towards Amazon that I am less and less inclined to use them. Recruit delivery drivers who actually respect the customer and their customer service, otherwise I have no alternative but to keep posting these negative reviews.

author: Sheena
text:
Amazon you just need to do better!!!!! I’m so over Amazon it’s not even funny!!! You have become so unreliable!!! Your delivery drivers don’t care about delivery instructions! I have an enclosed front porch and live on a busy street with easy access for people to run up and steal off my front steps! I have instructions to ask delivery people to please put my stuff on the actual porch, but your delivery people always leave my stuff sitting in the rain or snow! They put stuff on the side of my house that I always have to go look for or they deliver it to a house across the street, or even worse it’s delivered to a house on the other side of town! Maybe make sure your delivery people know how to read numbers let alone street names! 
Yesterday my order was out for delivery for 12 hours and now it’s not coming until tomorrow! Whhhhaaaatttt?!?! How’s it out for delivery and then it will take 2 extra days to be delivered?!?!?! 
Long gone are the good old days when you could place an order and everything was delivered the next day! I’m canceling everything I have with Amazon and will just start getting everything delivered by Walmart!!!
I can’t even seem to find anyone’s wish lists anymore! Why have you made it so difficult to shop for my family and friends?!?!
I know the several thousand dollars I will no longer be spending at Amazon annually won’t hurt Jeff Bezos but it’s worth it to me to not give y’all one more cent of my hard earned money, especially since I keep hearing about how awful you treat your employees who are responsible for you to keep raking in those billions!!!

author: Vafo Rubov
text:
I am beyond disgusted with how Amazon treats its paying Prime members. I recently discovered that even though I pay for a Prime subscription, I am being charged MORE for the same items than non-Prime users. To make it even worse, my delivery estimate was set for 5 days, while a regular account was offered next-day delivery for the exact same product.

This is a complete scam and a total betrayal of customer trust. They take your membership fee and then manipulate prices and shipping speeds to their advantage. It is a fraudulent system designed to rip people off. I have already canceled my membership and will never trust this company again. If you think Prime saves you money or time, think again. You are being lied to!

author: wenkexin zhang
text:
Very poor and confusing delivery experience.

I placed a Prime order and received an email on 12 May saying my parcel was ready for collection. I went to the Amazon Counter/Collect+ collection point with my barcode, but the shop staff scanned it, searched for the parcel, and told me it was not there.

Since then, four days have passed and the tracking status has kept changing between “ready for collection”, “arriving today”, and “running late”. I also received reminder emails telling me to collect the parcel, even though the collection point had already confirmed they did not have it.

I contacted Amazon customer service multiple times and was given inconsistent information. One adviser said the parcel was being redelivered, another said it had not been delivered yet, and later I was told it may have been dropped at the store but still not marked as delivered.

This has wasted my time and caused unnecessary confusion. The tracking information and collection emails were unreliable, and I had to request a refund because the parcel could not be collected as promised.
